item.title: Our Allies Are Wondering Whether Supporting the American War Machine Is Worth It
item.description: Is the Iran war an early sign of “superpower suicide"?

item.title: Danny McBride Thinks Men Learned All the Wrong Lessons From Movies
item.description: The writer and actor, known for his profane comedic antiheroes, likes to find universal truths in human flaws.

item.title: I’m Worried My Friends’ Son May Harm Them. What Should I Do?
What I Learned About Masculinity at Thai Kickboxing School
item.description: A week inside a grueling Muay Thai training program in Phuket.

item.title: The Teenage Soldier Who Revealed How the Patriots Really Won the War
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Patriot Housewife Whose Plays Helped Push America Toward Revolution
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Man of Faith Who Heard a Righteous Call in the Founding Credo
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Enslaved Woman Who Sued for Freedom and Emancipated Herself
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: Robby Hoffman Will Always Feel Poor, No Matter How Rich She Gets
item.description: The comedian and actor says class and the way she grew up inform everything about the way she lives now.

item.title: My Awful Mother Is Getting Alzheimer’s. What Do I Owe Her?
item.description: The thought of having her live with me makes me physically sick.

item.title: Should You Let Your Dog Fertilize Your Lawn?
item.description: A ruling on a dispute over proper mowing technique.

item.title: Should My Husband Tell His Best Friend That We Can’t Stand His Wife?
item.description: His friend has accused him of being distant, even hinting that he’s a social climber for not wanting to hang out with them.

item.title: Is There a Founding Story That Can Unify Left and Right?
item.description: There has never been agreement over the meaning of America’s creation 250 years ago. Maybe there shouldn’t be.

item.title: Visions of America: The Revolution as You’ve Never Seen It Before
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.
